data.title: Cayman's Oxbridge Re swings to Q4 profit
data.description:
Overview

  • Cayman Islands tokenized reinsurance platform's Q4 net premiums earned fell. Q4 Net income swung to profit

  • Full-year net loss narrowed as higher revenues offset increased expenses and reinsurance losses

  • Combined ratio rose to 264.1% for 2025, reflecting higher expenses and hurricane-related losses

Outlook

  • Oxbridge targets 20% and 42% annual returns for T20 and T42 tokens in 2026–2027

  • Company says El Niño may reduce hurricane activity for 2026 Atlantic season

  • Oxbridge plans to expand tokenization to data center revenues and AI infrastructure assets

Result Drivers

  • PREMIUM RATE DECLINE - Net premiums earned fell due to lower weighted average rates on reinsurance contracts in force during the quarter

  • HURRICANE LOSSES - Higher expenses and loss ratio driven by losses on reinsurance contracts affected by Hurricane Milton

  • HIGHER OPERATING COSTS - Expense ratio rose due to increased professional, marketing, tokenization, and legal costs

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

FY Combined Ratio

264.10%

FY Expense Ratio

144.20%

Analyst Coverage

  • The one available analyst rating on the shares is "buy"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the reinsurance peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Oxbridge Re Holdings Ltd is $5.00, about 463.1% above its March 27 closing price of $0.89

data.title: Boston Omaha fiscal 2025 revenue rises
data.description:
Overview

  • US holding company reported fiscal 2025 revenue rose yr/yr about 6% to $114.4 mln

  • Net loss attributable to common stockholders widened to $12.4 mln for fiscal 2025

  • Company repurchased 444,753 shares for $5.8 mln during fiscal 2025

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance or forecasts for the current quarter or full year in the press release

Result Drivers

  • INVESTMENT LOSSES - Co said net other expense included a $17.6 mln unrealized loss on Sky Harbour warrants and a $6.9 mln loss from changes in fair value of fund assets

  • INCOME FROM AFFILIATES - $6.5 mln in income from unconsolidated affiliates mainly related to equity method position in Sky Harbour partially offset losses

data.title: UBS Cuts Year-End Target for Australia's Benchmark ASX 200 — Market Talk
data.description: UBS lowers its year-end target for Australia's benchmark S&P/ASX 200 index. It cites a likelihood that inflation spikes and economic growth slows, but the country avoids a recession. UBS now expects the ASX 200 to end the year at 8800, compared to a prior forecast of 9400. The ASX 200 ended Monday at 8461. "Although earnings downgrades will likely dominate through the next quarter, markets react fast nowadays, and prices will choose to 'look through' to the other side," strategist Richard Schellbach says. "The EPS downgrades we expect over coming months should see the pace of profit growth decelerate." UBS's new 8800 target would mean the ASX 200 ends 2026 roughly flat for the year. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Repay Holdings: Agreed to Acquire Kubra for Approximately $372M >RPAY
data.description: This news item displays a headline only and has no other text.

data.title: REPAY Announces Agreement to Acquire KUBRA
data.description:
(22:00 GMT) *REPAY Announces Agreement To Acquire KUBRA >RPAY
(22:00 GMT) Press Release: REPAY Announces Agreement to Acquire KUBRA

Combination creates a Scaled Consumer Bill Payment Provider

REPAY to host conference call tomorrow at 8:00 AM ET

ATLANTA--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 30, 2026--

Repay Holdings Corporation ("REPAY" or the "Company"), a leading provider of integrated payment processing solutions, today announced a definitive agreement to acquire Kubra Data Transfer LTD. ("KUBRA") for approximately $372 million. The acquisition will be financed with a combination of cash on hand and debt financing.

KUBRA is a leading provider of bill payment and customer communication management solutions serving some of the largest utility and government entities, touching over 40% of households in the US and Canada. KUBRA offers an embedded technology platform comprising six core solutions across multiple verticals. Their highly reoccurring business model serves over 250 clients today with a deeply integrated offering to ERP providers across their core verticals.

The transaction strengthens two complementary, vertical-leading businesses combining REPAY's extensive payment expertise and technology platform with KUBRA's attractive verticals, partnerships, and go-to-market approach. On a combined basis, the Company will have significant scale across diverse growth markets enabling over $130 billion in annual payment volumes.

"Today's announcement advances REPAY on our transformational journey to become a leading bill payment provider. The combination brings together highly complementary go-to-market approaches, creating robust opportunities to enhance growth, while also deepening client experiences and driving operational and financial efficiencies," said John Morris, Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer of REPAY.

"We are excited to enter KUBRA's next phase by joining REPAY and creating a scaled payments platform," said Rick Watkin, President and Chief Executive Officer of KUBRA. "REPAY will enhance value for our clients, while helping to further pursue growth opportunities in our end markets. I am thrilled about the opportunities this transaction provides for KUBRA and our team."

Compelling Strategic Rationale with Strong Value Creation Opportunities

  • Vertical Expansion: The acquisition of KUBRA adds attractive verticals such as utilities, government, and insurance. Going forward, go-to-market and sales teams will leverage the company's extensive payments capabilities, vertical expertise, and robust distribution channels across 18+ dynamic verticals.
  • Creates Scale: REPAY's payment platform will have significant scale with combined 20251 Revenue and Adjusted EBITDA of approximately $548 million and $178 million, respectively.
  • Value Creation Opportunities: The transaction is expected to generate approximately $15+ million of annual run-rate costs synergies and approximately $5+ million of technology savings over the next three years through combining operations, platform consolidation, and other scale efficiencies. REPAY expects the transaction to unlock additional value with expected revenue opportunities of approximately $5+ million by 2028 as REPAY benefits from offering bill presentment, communications services, a payment engine, and core processing solutions across all clients.
  • Ramping Accretive Profile: REPAY expects Free Cash Flow accretion2 of 25% by 2028.

Transaction Details

REPAY will acquire KUBRA in an all-cash transaction for approximately $372 million, which is subject to customary purchase price adjustments. The transaction will be funded with cash on hand and debt financing. In connection with the transaction, REPAY has received a debt commitment letter from Truist Bank for a $500 million term loan, along with a $100 million undrawn revolving credit facility. At closing, REPAY expects net leverage(3) of approximately 4.0x on a post-transaction basis and expects to reduce net leverage to below 3.0x within 18 months.

Timing and Approvals

The transaction is subject to regulatory approvals in the U.S. and Canada and certain customary closing conditions. REPAY expects the transaction to close in the second quarter of 2026.

Advisors

Truist Securities, Inc. served as exclusive financial advisor to REPAY and Truist Bank is providing committed financing to support the acquisition. Troutman Pepper Locke LLP served as legal advisor to REPAY. Financial Technology Partners served as exclusive financial advisor to KUBRA. Clifford Chance US LLP and the Hearst Office of General Counsel served as legal advisors to KUBRA and Hearst Corporation.

Conference Call

REPAY will host a conference call to discuss the acquisition on March 31, 2026 at 8:00 am ET. The call will be webcast live from REPAY's investor relations website at https://investors.repay.com/investor-relations. The conference call can also be accessed live over the phone by dialing (877) 407-3982, or for international callers (201) 493-6780. A replay will be available one hour after the call and can be accessed by dialing (844) 512-2921 or (412) 317-6671 for international callers; the conference ID is 13759307. The replay will be available at https://investors.repay.com/investor-relations.

Non-GAAP Financial Measures

This report includes certain non-GAAP financial measures that management uses to evaluate the Company's operating business, measure performance, and make strategic decisions, including Adjusted EBITDA, Free Cash Flow accretion and net leverage, as well as certain forward-looking projections that are not reconcilable with GAAP measures due to their inherent uncertainty. Free Cash Flow is a non-GAAP financial measure that represents net cash flow provided by operating activities less total capital expenditures. Net leverage is a non-GAAP financial measure calculated by dividing total debt (less cash and cash equivalents) divided by Adjusted EBITDA. Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP financial measure that represents net income prior to interest expense, tax expense, depreciation and amortization, as adjusted to add back certain charges deemed to not be part of normal operating expenses, non-cash charges and/or non-recurring charges, such as gain on extinguishment of debt, non-cash impairment loss, non-cash change in fair value of assets and liabilities, share-based compensation charges, transaction expenses, restructuring and other strategic initiative costs, gain on extinguishment of debt and other non-recurring charges. REPAY does not provide quantitative reconciliation of forward-looking, non-GAAP financial measures to the most directly comparable GAAP financial measure because it is difficult to reliably predict or estimate the relevant components without unreasonable effort due to future uncertainties that may potentially have significant impact on such calculations, and providing them may imply a degree of precision that would be confusing or potentially misleading. REPAY believes that Adjusted EBITDA, Free Cash Flow accretion and net leverage provide useful information to investors and others in understanding and evaluating its operating results in the same manner as management. However, these non-GAAP financial measures are not financial measures calculated in accordance with GAAP and should not be considered as a substitute for net income, operating profit, net cash provided by operating activities, or any other operating performance measure calculated in accordance with GAAP. Using these non-GAAP financial measures to analyze REPAY's business has material limitations because the calculations are based on the subjective determination of management regarding the nature and classification of events and circumstances that investors may find significant. In addition, although other companies in REPAY's industry may report measures titled as the same or similar measures, such non-GAAP financial measures may be calculated differently from how REPAY calculates its non-GAAP financial measures, which reduces their overall usefulness as comparative measures. Because of these limitations, you should consider REPAY's non-GAAP financial measures alongside other financial performance measures, including net income, net cash provided by operating activities and REPAY's other financial results presented in accordance with GAAP.

Forward-Looking Statements

This communication contains "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Such statements include, but are not limited to, statements about future financial and operating results, REPAY's plans, objectives, expectations and intentions with respect to future operations, products and services; and other statements identified by words such as "can," "may," "will," "expect," "anticipate," "estimate," "believe," "projection" or words of similar meaning. These forward-looking statements include, but are not limited to: anticipated benefits from, and the expected timing for completion of, the KUBRA acquisition, expected strengthening of REPAY's product offering, future market, growth and synergy opportunities, payment volume, net leverage and Free Cash Flow estimates, and the level of KUBRA's expected growth and financial contributions, including revenue and Adjusted EBITDA. Such forward-looking statements are based upon the current beliefs and expectations of REPAY's management and are inherently subject to significant business, economic and competitive uncertainties and contingencies, many of which are difficult to predict and generally beyond our control.

In addition to factors disclosed in REPAY's reports filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, including its Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025, and those identified elsewhere in this communication, the following factors, among others, could cause actual results and the timing of events to differ materially from the anticipated results or other expectations expressed in the forward-looking statements: the risk that the proposed transaction may not be completed in a timely manner or at all; the inability to integrate and/or realize the benefits of the KUBRA acquisition, including expected synergies; the occurrence of any fact, event, change, development or circumstance that could give rise to the termination of the definitive acquisition agreement; the failure to satisfy any of the conditions to the consummation of the transaction, including the receipt of certain governmental or regulatory approvals; the risk that the financing necessary to consummate the transaction may not be obtained, may be delayed, or may be available only on less favorable terms than anticipated; that the announcement of the proposed acquisition could disrupt REPAY's or KUBRA's relationships with customers, employees or other business partners; changes in the bill payment and payment processing markets in which REPAY and KUBRA operate, including with respect to the applicable competitive landscape, technology evolution or regulatory changes; changes in the vertical markets that REPAY or KUBRA target, including the regulatory environment applicable to those customers; risks relating to REPAY's and KUBRA's relationships within the payment ecosystem; and risks relating to data security.

Actual results, performance or achievements may differ materially, and potentially adversely, from any projections and forward-looking statements and the assumptions on which those forward-looking statements are based. There can be no assurance that the data contained herein is reflective of future performance to any degree. You are cautioned not to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements as a predictor of future performance. All information set forth herein speaks only as of the date hereof in the case of information about REPAY or the date of such information in the case of information from persons other than REPAY, and REPAY disclaims any intention or obligation to update any forward-looking statements as a result of developments occurring after the date of this communication. Forecasts and estimates regarding REPAY's industry and end markets are based on sources it believes to be reliable, however there can be no assurance these forecasts and estimates will prove accurate in whole or in part. Combined, projected and estimated numbers are used for illustrative purpose only, are not forecasts and may not reflect actual results.

About REPAY

REPAY provides integrated payment processing solutions to verticals that have specific transaction processing needs. REPAY's proprietary, integrated payment technology platform reduces the complexity of electronic payments for clients, while enhancing the overall experience for consumers and businesses.

About KUBRA

KUBRA, founded in 1992 and headquartered in Mississauga, Ontario, is an industry-leading provider of customer experience management solutions to some of the largest utility, government, and insurance entities in North America. KUBRA's platform offering includes billing and payments, alerts and preference management, artificial intelligence solutions, mobile apps, and utility mapping solutions. KUBRA reaches over 40% of households in the United States and Canada, providing performance-driven value to more than 250 clients and their customers.

____________________________
(1) Combined 2025 financial metrics are based on reported REPAY plus KUBRA
Revenue of approximately $239 million and Adjusted EBITDA of approximately $49
million. Adjusted EBITDA is a non-GAAP measure. See "Non-GAAP Financial
Measures" herein for additional information.
(2) Free Cash Flow accretion is a non-GAAP measure. See "Non-GAAP Financial
Measures" herein for additional information.
(3) Net leverage at close includes transaction-related adjustments and
synergies. Net leverage is a non-GAAP financial measure. See "Non-GAAP
Financial Measures" herein for additional information.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260329476191/en/

data.title: East West Bancorp Announces Dates for Second Quarter and Third Quarter 2026 Earnings Calls, June Conference Participation
data.description:
(22:00 GMT) *East West Bancorp 1Q Total Assets $80.4B >EWBC
(22:00 GMT) Press Release: East West Bancorp Announces Dates for Second Quarter and Third Quarter 2026 Earnings Calls, June Conference Participation

PASADENA, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 30, 2026--

East West Bancorp, Inc. ("East West" or the "Company") , announced plans to host conference calls to review financial results on the following dates:

 
Financial Period     Earnings Release Date      Conference Call Time 
Second Quarter 2026  Tuesday, July 21, 2026     2 p.m. PT/5 p.m. ET 
Third Quarter 2026   Tuesday, October 20, 2026  2 p.m. PT/5 p.m. ET

The financial results are scheduled to be released after the market closes on the dates noted above. The live audio webcast, earnings release, and earnings presentation will be available on the Investor Relations site at www.eastwestbank.com/investors. Information to access a replay of the call will be available one hour after the call on the Investor Relations site at www.eastwestbank.com/investors. To access the conference call from locations within the United States please dial 877-506-6399. Participants calling from Canada should dial 855-669-9657, while participants calling from an international location should dial 412-902-6699.

Additionally, East West announced today that management will meet with investors at two investor conferences in June:

  • Morgan Stanley US Financials Conference on Tuesday, June 9th and Wednesday, June 10th, 2026
  • Citi's 2026 Regional Bank Day on Thursday, June 11th, 2026.

Management will deliver remarks during a fireside chat on Wednesday, June 10(th) at 10:30 a.m. Eastern Time at the Morgan Stanley Conference. The remarks and presentation may include forward-looking statements. The live audio webcast, presentation, and replay will be available on the Events and Presentations page of East West's Investor Relations site at www.eastwestbank.com/investors.

About East West

East West provides financial services that help customers reach further and connect to new opportunities. East West Bancorp, Inc. is a public company with total assets of $80.4 billion as of December 31, 2025. The Company's wholly-owned subsidiary, East West Bank, is the largest independent bank headquartered in Southern California, and operates over 110 locations in the United States and Asia. The Bank's markets in the United States include California, Georgia, Illinois, Massachusetts, Nevada, New York, Texas, and Washington. For more information on East West, visit www.eastwestbank.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260330106194/en/

CONTACT: FOR INVESTOR INQUIRIES, CONTACT:

Adrienne Atkinson

Director of Investor Relations and Corporate Development

T: (626) 788-7536

E: adrienne.atkinson@eastwestbank.com

data.title: Apro (AT) - APRO AI Oracle & Lucy AI - 30 March 2026
data.description:
According to the APRO team, LucyOSAI has integrated APRO’s AI Oracle to give autonomous agents verifiable on-chain access to multiple off-chain data verticals (crypto, sports, esports, books, and social signals). Mechanically, this expands APRO’s oracle from price feeds toward a broader AI-data layer where agents trigger on-chain actions based on authenticated external data. If LucyOSAI gains traction as an “agentic OS for crypto,” APRO could see higher query volumes and integration depth, supporting oracle fee revenue and reinforcing its positioning in the AI + on-chain agents narrative, which can attract speculative flows around the AT token.

APRO |NO.1 Oracle
@APRO_Oracle

One of the most exciting use cases yet! @LucyOSAI — the agentic operating system for crypto — has officially integrated APRO's AI Oracle.

Not just crypto prices.

But any cutting-edge data source you can imagine: sports, esports, books, social signals, and beyond — all… https://t.co/NME0uPkKaJ

Mar 30, 2026

data.title: Aave (AAVE) - X Layer - 30 March 2026
data.description:
Aave has been deployed on X Layer, the OKX-affiliated L2 network, making Aave money markets directly accessible within the OKX ecosystem, as confirmed in the official announcement. Mechanically, this adds a new venue where users can supply and borrow assets through Aave, potentially tapping OKX’s existing user base and liquidity. More chains generally mean higher protocol TVL, fee generation, and governance relevance for AAVE, but the real impact will depend on X Layer’s user growth, incentive programs, and asset listings. Traders should monitor TVL and borrowing rates on X Layer to gauge whether this deployment materially boosts Aave usage.

Aave
@aave

Aave is live on @XLayerOfficial and is now part of the @okx ecosystem. pic.twitter.com/xjI2F712yA

Mar 30, 2026

data.title: iExec RLC (RLC) - Confidential Token Demo - 30 March 2026
data.description:
iExec has released a demo of its Confidential Token on Arbitrum Sepolia, featuring programmable privacy with selective disclosure, encrypted balances, and encrypted transfers, as outlined in their announcement. The mechanism combines token logic with secure off-chain computation to hide sensitive state while allowing compliance-friendly disclosures when needed. If brought to mainnet and adopted, this could make iExec infrastructure more attractive for institutions and privacy-focused DeFi projects, potentially increasing demand for RLC to pay for confidential computing resources and incentivize ecosystem participants. Price impact will hinge on actual mainnet rollout and real user adoption beyond the testnet demo.

iExec RLC EthCC
@iEx_ec

Introducing the iExec Confidential Token.

Demo live on @Arbitrum Sepolia.

Mainnet soon.

Experience Programmable Privacy for Web3:

Selective disclosure

Encrypted balances

Encrypted transfers pic.twitter.com/lKiReHsb4R

Mar 30, 2026

data.title: Global Arena posts $0.399M revenue, $(0.00) EPS on Q1 2025 10-Q
data.description:
Global Arena reported first-quarter 2025 revenue of $0.399 million, up 72.0% year-over-year primarily due to increased elections activity, while diluted loss per share was $(0.00) on a net loss of $(0.288) million. Operating income swung to an operating loss of $(0.077) million from prior-year operating income, driven by higher operating expenses. The company continues to scale operations and pursue certification and sales opportunities tied to its voting platforms.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.399 million, increase of 72.0% versus prior year quarter.
  • Operating Income: $(0.077) million (operating loss).
  • Net Income / Net Loss: $(0.288) million net loss.
  • Net loss attributable to Global Arena: $(0.288) million.
  • Loss per share – basic and diluted: $(0.00); weighted average shares outstanding 1,695,351,226.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Higher revenues were driven by increased elections activity.
  • Platform deployment: Expanded deployment of proprietary mail, in-person and AWS-hosted online voting platforms.
  • Operational scaling: Increased headcount and use of temporary staff for special elections; higher printing and tabulation costs incurred to support expanded election activity.
  • Product capabilities: Continued production use of OMR/OCR/barcode scanning, triple-audit tabulation and AES-256/RSA-4096 encryption.
  • Certification and sales process: Pursuing U.S. federal and state voting system certifications and engaged in negotiated asset sale discussions.

Original SEC Filing: Global Arena Holding, Inc. [ GAHC ] - 10-Q - Mar. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Village Farms Extends FCC Credit Facility to 2031, Cuts Interest Margin by 50 Bps
data.description:
Village Farms amended its Amended and Restated Credit Agreement with Farm Credit Canada, extending the maturity to February 3, 2031 and reducing the applicable interest margin by 50 basis points. The amendment is expected to lower borrowing costs and enhance financial flexibility. Management indicated the updated terms support the company's long-term liquidity needs.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to Amended and Restated Credit Agreement
  • Counterparty: Farm Credit Canada
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 27 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through Feb 03 2031
  • Reason: Extend maturity and lower borrowing costs

data.title: VerifyMe Reports 2025 Results: $16.4M Revenue, $4.9M Net Loss, $4.4M Cash
data.description:
VerifyMe reported full-year 2025 revenue of $16.4 million and a net loss of $4.9 million, with cash of $4.4 million and a $2.0 million short-term note receivable as of December 31, 2025. Fourth-quarter revenue was $2.4 million with gross profit of $1.2 million (49% margin) and a Q4 net loss of $0.7 million. Adjusted EBITDA for 2025 was $1.0 million compared to $0.9 million in 2024.

Financial Highlights

  • Full-year revenue: $16.4 million for 2025, compared to $24.2 million in 2024.
  • Full-year gross profit: $6.3 million (39%) in 2025, compared to $8.7 million (36%) in 2024; Q4 gross profit $1.2 million (49%).
  • Full-year net loss: $4.9 million in 2025 (including $4.3 million of one-time adjustments); Q4 net loss $0.7 million (loss per diluted share $0.05).
  • Adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP): $1.0 million for 2025, compared to $0.9 million in 2024; Q4 Adjusted EBITDA ($0.1) million.
  • Balance sheet: Cash of $4.4 million and short-term note receivable of $2.0 million as of December 31, 2025; working capital $5.7 million.

Business Highlights

  • Transition of logistics relationships: Began moving ProActive clients from the prior shipping partner to a new strategic shipping partner during Q4 2025 and continued transitions into 2026.
  • Premium client contracts: By Q1 2026, remaining Premium clients were transitioned to contracts directly with VerifyMe rather than through the former shipping partner.
  • Operational impact of partner change: Approximately 78% of the Q4 year-over-year revenue reduction was attributable to termination of the prior carrier partnership, which eroded the customer base.
  • Strategic transaction: Entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger to combine with Open World, Ltd.; intends shareholder call after filing associated Form S-4 registration and proxy materials.
  • Other corporate actions: Terminated ATM Sales Agreement with Roth Capital Partners and continued cost reduction and pricing improvements under the new shipping partner arrangement.

data.title: Immix completes enrollment in NEXICART-2; topline results expected Q3 2026
data.description:

  • Immix onboarded Richard Graydon, MD, PhD as Chief Medical Officer to support clinical development and BLA submissions.
  • NXC-201 holds FDA Breakthrough Therapy and RMAT designations and orphan drug status in the US and EU.

data.title: ECD Automotive Design Sells $395,860 Additional Senior Secured Convertible Notes to Parent, Maturing 2026
data.description:
ECD Automotive Design expanded its June 2025 financing by selling an additional $395,859.66 in senior secured convertible notes to its parent institutional investor, effective Mar 23, 2026. The purchase price was $360,326.85, and the notes mature on Dec 12, 2026, unless earlier converted or redeemed. A 9.99% beneficial ownership cap applies, and at the $0.0034 floor price, the notes would be convertible into 116,429,312 shares. The company expects the transaction to bolster liquidity and financial flexibility.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Senior secured convertible notes purchase under June 2025 securities purchase agreement
  • Counterparty: Institutional investor (parent company)
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 24 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until Dec 12 2026
  • Reason: Strengthen liquidity and financial flexibility

Original SEC Filing: ECD Automotive Design, Inc. [ ECDA ] - 8-K - Mar. 30, 2026

data.title: Alset to Lend $2.45M to DSS in 3% Five-Year Convertible Note; Warrants for 16.6M Shares
data.description:
Alset, through majority-owned subsidiary Alset International, entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement with DSS to provide a $2.45 million loan in exchange for a convertible promissory note and warrants. The note bears 3% simple interest and is convertible at $0.74 per share any time before its five-year maturity. Alset will also receive warrants to purchase up to 16,554,055 DSS shares at $0.93 per share, expiring in five years. Closing is subject to customary conditions including DSS stockholder approval.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Securities purchase agreement for 3% five-year convertible note and stock warrants
  • Counterparty: DSS
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 26 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years
  • Reason: Provide financing with potential equity upside in affiliate

data.title: Falcon's Beyond Global 2025 10-K: Revenue $14.9M; Net Income $6.3M
data.description:
Falcon's Beyond Global reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $14.9 million, driven by new attractions contracts and product sales, and recorded net income of $6.3 million compared with a significantly higher prior-year result that included an earnout fair-value gain. The company posted a loss from operations of $(13.408) million, an improvement versus 2024, while per-share net income figures were not provided in the excerpts. These results reflect increased project activity and recent strategic acquisitions to scale attraction design and manufacturing capabilities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $14.896 million, an increase of $8.151 million versus 2024 driven by new attractions contracts.
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations of $(13.408) million, improved $2.459 million versus 2024.
  • Net Income: $6.312 million, declined $143.169 million versus 2024 due to a prior-year earnout fair-value gain.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not provided in the Part II Item 7/Item 8 excerpts.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth and Channels: Revenue rose to $14.9 million in 2025 from $6.7 million in 2024, primarily driven by new attractions contracts and product sales after launching Falcon’s Attractions following the OES acquisition.
  • Acquisition and Capacity Expansion: Acquired OES intellectual property, assets and a 103,000+ sq ft R&D/manufacturing facility to scale attraction services, integration, design and manufacturing capabilities.
  • Joint Venture Activity: The PDP joint venture realized a $30 million gain from the Tenerife sale and continues operations in Mallorca, contributing to cash distributions and supporting liquidity.
  • R&D and Project Execution: Increased R&D and project build activity to develop location-based experiences; revenues and timing are subject to project schedules and rollout cadence.

Original SEC Filing: Falcon's Beyond Global, Inc. [ FBYD ] - 10-K - Mar. 30, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: DeFi Development 10-K: $11.4M Revenue, $(4.00) EPS on Net Loss of $(73.8)M
data.description:
DeFi Development reported consolidated revenue of $11.386 million for the year, driven primarily by staking rewards, while net loss widened to $(73.788) million, or $(4.00) per share, largely due to impairments and derivative losses. Operating loss increased substantially to $(36.398) million as a result of higher general and administrative expenses and digital asset activities. The results reflect a strategic pivot toward Solana-focused treasury and validator operations alongside continued legacy platform activity.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $11.386 million (consolidated), increased $9.286 million (442.2%) driven by digital asset staking rewards.
  • Operating (loss) income: $(36.398) million; operating loss widened $33.395 million versus prior year due to G&A and digital asset activities.
  • Net (loss) income: $(73.788) million; net loss increased $71.061 million primarily from impairments and derivative losses.
  • Net (loss) income per share: $(4.00), basic and diluted; weighted-average shares outstanding: 18,453 thousand.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth driver: Rapid increase in staking rewards from a newly established SOL-focused treasury.
  • Strategic pivot: In 2025 the company shifted its primary strategy to accumulate and actively manage SOL and SOL-related assets and to operate Solana validators.
  • Brand and corporate actions: Rebranded to DeFi Development, changed ticker to DFDV, and adopted a treasury policy prioritizing long-term SOL exposure.
  • Operational milestones: Acquired two validator nodes and launched digital asset treasury operations, expanding staking and validator revenue streams.
  • Legacy platform activity: Continued Real Estate Platform operations with modest SaaS growth and divested Janover Pro, reducing future subscription revenue.

Original SEC Filing: DeFi Development Corp. [ DFDV ] - 10-K - Mar. 30, 2026

Disclaimer
